reviews.review_text:
Allow me to preface this with some clarification: I like this game, I like it a lot, but I don't think it's nearly as satisfying to play as the first game, this game is great in most aspects, but it has 1 glaring issue:

It's just too damn big.

The maps are almost twice as large as Hotline Miami 1, and if they aren't, they have multiple floors or stages or some ♥♥♥♥, and it makes the game feel so much worse as a result, in Hotline Miami 1 I can run from room to room punching ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ and doing executions without stopping, in HM2, because of all the sight lines, I have to stand out in the open for a second just to have a grunt run 5 miles to me so I can punch him and then repeat the process 50 more times, it feels awful.

Hotline Miami 2 is an extremely ambitious game that has also really earned my respect.

The story itself is far more ambitious than the first game as there are multiple different characters with intersecting storylines told in a non-linear fashion, requiring attention to piece together what it all meant. This approach also gives a lot more varied gameplay and different level theming to experience. I think the ending was perfect given what the franchise set out to do, and I found myself thinking about the game as a whole many times already after beating it. I'll certainly be revisiting its story as I feel there will be even more details to pick up with each read.

Most of my praise from the first game is bolstered in the second - the commentary on violence in video games, the usage of many different artists to give a very varied soundtrack, and how fun it is to approach every level as a puzzle. It's a natural progression of the game's mechanics in many aspects, a natural evolution.

I view this game very positively, however I do agree with the common criticism that the level design took a step back. Levels are far bigger and much more open, but having the same one hit to die mechanic made these levels far more frustrating to tackle than they should have been. Most levels also give a choice of masks (abilities) which were a cool mechanic in the first game, but in the sequel some masks make certain levels feel unbeatable.

In other words I view Hotline Miami 2 as a game that took two steps forward in terms of storytelling, but one step backwards in terms of gameplay. It's still a natural evolution of the first game and is absolutely worth playing through and through.

A top down version of doom where you eliminate soviet dudes and you both are one shot. I COULDN'T ASK FOR MORE!!!
Story wise it was good and it ended in an unexpected way but overall I think it was good.
Gameplay wise tho it wasn't that diff from Miami 1 but still a few improvement could be felt here and there.
I hear people complain about playing with melee in this game and it being down graded, I don't know their use and play style of melees in this game but personally I was having a great time with melees in this game

You will never get tired of this game because of workshop missions, man you gotta love those workshop missions some of them are true masterpieces. ( I played this game for like 16hrs but since most of it was in offline mode it didn't count)

The first game was already good, but I enjoyed this one more. The different characters make the gameplay more interesting, the story is incredible once pieced together and the music just hits harder here. If you liked the first game then going for the sequel here is a no-brainer.

100% achievement completion has you do a little bit of secret hunting, scoring A+ ranks on every normal stage and beating hard mode. All of these are great fun, but there's one experience killer in the bunch - grinding 50k kills. You can beat everything the game throws at you multiple times and you'll be about 10% there. There is a decent grind spot for this, but it's a draining grind nonetheless.

I think this game is better than the first one just not in the gameplay. I mean sure they have new weapons and stuff but man does it feel unfair sometimes but the story is way better and ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ roller mobster is peak

This game is so hard and frustrating that I can’t help but love it.

The satisfaction you get after beating each level is absolutely real, and tbh, it’s a great sequel to the first game.

Lore-wise, it’s amazing. I’ve always loved games that play with time, like Max Payne 2, where everything slowly becomes clear by the end and in your second playthrough you would understand a lot while playing.
This game is definitely one of those masterpieces.

That said, some levels take way too long to beat, and at some point it starts feeling exhausting.

Overall, this game gave me an incredible mental breakdown.
Peak, 10/10. The game knows what it wants to be and nails it in every way.
It's a good game. That said, Jacob Geller is 100% right with his critiques.

Story is hard to follow, but the atmosphere makes up for it. Down right addictive gameplay. Love the over the top pixel gore and animations.

I've almost 100%'d this game on my Ps4, I got it on Steam for the level editor. it's one of the best video game sequels of all time in my opinion. You're going to ahte it on your first playthrough, but after replaying again and again I only enjoy the game more and more. it doesn't change or remove anything, it only adds to the formula and is far more fleshed out than the first game.
10/10 for the game itself. would recommend. my only complaint is that there's no controller support for PlayStation controllers. it's a twin-stick shooter, I need my sticks! Had to get an Xbox controller especially for this game.

Lore-wise, it's much better than the first game, gameplay-wise, it's far inferior. The levels are much longer than in the previous game, making much of the game tedious. In addition, the mask mechanic is underutilized, appearing only in a few levels, and the levels are inconsistent, often being very easy in one and extremely difficult in the next, and vice versa.
The story of this game expands much more on the story of the first and offers an ending that I at least consider satisfactory. The game in general is very well written, I won't go into details otherwise the review will become too long.
It's not a bad game, but it's certainly inferior to the first. Still, I recommend it if you liked the first one.

This game is a masterpiece. Coolest top-down shooter I've ever played. Soundtrack is incredible! Story is like a puzzle. You have to connect the dots as you go through the story. It's very chaotic at first, but at the end, it all snaps!

  10/10 video game!

I absolutely love the gameplay, it's addictive in it simplicity and extreme pacing. Every attack feels meaty, every kill feels earned, and the gameplay loop makes hours dissolve into minutes. But despite all of this, the game feels lost. It's as if the entire thing is a poorly-done translation, or a recovered set of tapes with half of the information scrubbed off throughout the years of rewinds. It's all vague, directionless, clearly trying to say something but amounting to a disheveled lump of semi-related plot points that trail off and meander around any type of deeper commentary or meaningful point. You can easily pick up on inspirations -- slashers, horror movies, Miami Vice and David Lynch -- but substance behind this inspiration is few and far-between. I'm certain there's some kind of greater commentary to be found with a lot of effort, but dissecting a game like this feels incredibly counter-productive, especially when that time is better spent immersed in the frustratingly-intoxicating gameplay loop. Enjoy the gory hyper-violence and don't think too hard about the mess of red herrings and unnecessarily-confused timeskips that tries to justify its existence.

BOIIIIIII DO U KNOW WHAT TIME IT ISSS jk lol.


 being fr this game is peak but i would heavily recommend playing hotline miami 1 1st as this is EXTRAORDINARILY hard. as well as the entire story not making any sense as basically every story is conected to either [spoiler] jacket or 50 blessings [/spoiler]
i loved this game so much even with its jankness that i decided to complete all achievements making this the first game i ever got all achievements on, maybe i do like hurting other people.

Overall fun, feels a lot diffirent then the first game, difficult but a good challenge its one of those games where you take a deep breath in ur chair after you cleared a level you have been stuck on for a bit. Love it
Hotline Miami 1 is incredible and a must play. But this game is so difficult that it's not any fun.

Hotline Miami 2 is nothing short of a masterpiece, but for far bigger reasons than it being a really fun top-down arcade shooter, no the reason this game is incredible and I can never stop thinking about it is that it is a shining example of developers having principles and sticking to them, to this end Hotline Miami 2 is so good expressly because it HATES its audience.
lets start with the gameplay, the game ramps up the difficulty of the first ten fold over its playtime, not feeling fit to hand you the game's violence on a silver platter it makes you damn near memorize levels from top to bottom near the end in order to succeed.
But where this game's themes shine most are in its characters, rather than quick witted vagabonds or troubled complex anti-heros, most of this game's playable characters are all deplorable, pathetic, or some mix of both, and they are explicit reflections of its players.
The Fans, the games would be protagonists are a bunch of druggy sex pests who murder simply because they get a kick out it, and their violence is rewarded with being unceremoniously killed at the halfway mark, the rest of the game is a somber attempt to pick up the pieces when its neon colored illusion is shattered before your very eyes.
Evan is one of the more sympathetic characters, he very comedically attempts to avoid violence in a game caked with it, showing how someone would ACTUALLY act were they dropped in a pulpy action movie, despite what they would probably say. Evan is a theory head, he wants to figure out the truth behind 50 Blessings, not a bad goal no doubt, but he ultimately misses the forest for the trees, and along with everyone else, doesn't realize the bigger picture until it is too late.
The game's ending is what finally ties everything together, rather than pamper the player with an upbeat or pleasant send off, you are met with complete and total nuclear annihilation, everything you fought tooth and nail for and against is turned to dust in an instant. The only two characters who get an even remotely good ending are those who decide to punch their ticket out of the violence and spend what little time they don't realize they have left with their family, those being Richter and Evan(and only if you choose for him to abandon his book). This all amounts to a commendable example of artists putting their foot down and confronting their audiences valorization of violence with a game that leaves you enriched rather than simply running high off of dopamine.
I'm so glad this game will NEVER get a sequel. 8.1/10
I have over 120+ hours on both HM1 and HM2 on my xbox and almost all achievements unlocked on HM2 on xbox, this game is awesome

This game is saved by the level editor and some really good additions but the AI and level design and lack of options is a huge step down from the first. If you want to make a metal gear, make a metal gear clone instead of making every guy with a gun a jackal sniper. Play Wild Thing and White House

Really cool story, Unfun Gameplay. 

The story is what most people know this game for. It spans three time periods that all influence the story of the whole game, with the Hawaiian war being where the violence really started. 

I didn't come to this game for just a story though, I also came to play a video game and this just was not fun. The obvious part of the levels is that they want you to use guns. If you go into it knowing that, it's tolerable. Level design really ruins the whole game though. The levels are too big and you can be killed by enemies that you could not see easily. Level 24 really pissed me off with the massive hallways and gunmen that I could not easily see. 

I did not hate this game, but it is a major step down from Hotline Miami 1 in terms of gameplay. It's bad enough that the story does not have the gameplay. It's just not fun to play.
very good, would recommend for people who enjoy top-down games of any variety and people who enjoy games with sick-ass storylines and graphics [EXTREMELY VIOLENT]
this game is like an abusive relationship. it's miserable but i can't stop playing

This game is very good. Try not to go into it with expectations from the first one. This one is good in different ways, I'd say look at it for what it is--not what it isn't. If you desperately want an apple, it's easy be mad if someone gives you an orange. Learn to enjoy the orange. I really enjoy the first game and this game too, but for different reasons. And I wholeheartedly encourage anyone to give this game a shot.

This simply isn't a fun game to play. It takes all the amazing ideas of the first, and throws them out. People have gone on long rants to me about the story, but never do they mention the gameplay, because any explanation of what's happening in minute-to-minute play ruins the facade created through pure scriptreading.
gotta get a grip before playing this specially the forest part with beard

also insanely repayable, although warning, this game is very ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ hard and I suggest you play the first game then this one.

Longer than the first game with tougher levels, and some neat new mechanics. Very good.

Absolutely one of the best games I've ever played. I owed it pirated for a while and I've played through it countless times. I'm achievement hunting now, and then I'm gonna learn to speedrun it. This game is what original top-down GTA wishes it was. Genuinely one of the best asthetics and soundtracks from any game. Simple. Fun. And has player made levels to endlessly play.
Great story, weird choices regarding mechanics and level design. Should've learnt more from it's older brother.

Greatly unique game. From the art style, top-down view, to the story being hidden all over the place, just like how it was in the original game. I'd say it even improved here, telling the stories of multiple characters, unlike the first game directly telling the story of two (Jacket and the biker).

Metal slug, Contra and Undertale in one game.

10/10

Probably my favorite indie game ever. HLM2 gets very ambitious with its story that has 13 playable characters that all connect to the story of the last game with most of them having different gameplay styles. Gunplay and enemy ai is a big improvement here. The enemy was probably my most hated part of the first game with how inconsistent and broken it is, all that is mostly fixed here with a few bugs from time to time. The soundtrack for 2 is insane and a big contrast to the first game which got the blood pumping but it kept you focused at the same time. 2 takes those tracks from 1 and cranks it up to 100 to make you feel like you're on coke and you cannot stop until the level is done even if it pisses you off for an hour, playing Death Wish for the first time does things to you. Absolute worst part of this game is the level design and how big the levels are, really love when I get shot from 100 feet across the level from an enemy that was hiding in a plant. Hard mode makes this ♥♥♥♥ even worse where the maps are flipped 180 degree's with even less ammo and more annoying enemies. If the level design was like the first game it would damn near be a perfect sequel, still like it more than 1 though.

Hotline Miami 2 is a fantastic sequel that takes the action and the amazing soundtrack to a whole new level. The music is so addictive that it perfectly fits the fast gameplay, making the experience truly unforgettable.

The game is AMAZING. I absolutely loved the way it continued the first game, hits us with way more lore the first part didn't mention and maybe makes us understand the questions from Richard in Hotline Miami 1.
WITHOUT DOUBT this has to be the best game i've played so far
this is one of the best games ever the story is amazing the gameplay is fantastic if you can play thi game now
Hotline miami 2 is very fun and improves on the systems of the first game while adding new content to keep it fun

A very brutal shooter with multiple different ways to play and so many different varieties of fun. Also has one of the saddest yet most fascinating storylines that I've ever seen. Chronologically I would recommend that anyone considering the Hotline Miami series should play through HLM1.

Great game, same as the first one, but even more memorisation, and a way messier storyline, I will watch a youtube video on it to understand it.(read my Hotline miami review to understand what are the goods and bads of this game as they are quite similar)
